triggers.description: Request URL contains SQL UNION or injection metacharacters
Response body contains email addresses (user table data leaked)
SQLite error message exposed in response body

validation: Response contains user credentials (email, password) mixed into menu item fields

triggers.description: Search query parameter contains SQL injection metacharacters
Search response leaks email addresses from users table
Database error string in search response indicates injection

validation: Response contains rows with user data leaked through search results

triggers.description: Review comment contains HTML/script injection tags
Stored XSS payload returned unescaped in reviews response

validation: Stored payload returned in GET /api/reviews response and rendered in browser DOM via innerHTML, causing script execution

triggers.description: Import URL targets a private or metadata IP address
Response contains bytes_fetched field confirming outbound server request
Response body contains cloud instance metadata keys

validation: Response contains bytes_fetched > 0, and content from internal/metadata service is returned

trigger: Authenticated as user A (e.g., id=2), GET /api/orders/3 (belongs to user B id=3)

triggers.description: Order endpoint returns 200 allowing cross-user access (no ownership check)
Response contains user_id field exposing that order belongs to different user

validation: Response returns order data with user_id !== requesting user's session ID

triggers.description: Profile response exposes a password field for a different user
Profile response contains an email address for another user

validation: Response returns profile of user id=1 (admin) including password field

type: Broken Authentication / Broken Function Level Authorization
trigger: GET /admin/orders with header X-Admin-Token: lab-admin-bypass-token (unauthenticated, or any logged-in user without admin role)

triggers.description: Request includes X-Admin-Token header with any value (hardcoded token bypass)
Admin endpoint returns 200 without proper role verification
Admin users response includes password fields for all users

validation: Response returns full order/user list including passwords without proper role verification

triggers.description: Response JSON contains a password field with plaintext value
Response body contains what looks like a plaintext password value

validation: JSON response contains password field with plaintext password

triggers.description: Debug response exposes sensitive server internals: admin_token and session_key
Response body contains recognisable hardcoded secret values
Debug endpoint accessible without authentication (200 on unauthenticated request)

validation: Response contains session_key, admin_token, db_path, version, and runtime internals in plaintext

triggers.description: Registration URL contains role parameter set to a privileged value
Request body includes a role field set to a privileged value
Registration response confirms user was created (user_id present)

validation: New user is created with role=admin. Verified by fetching /api/user/profile?id=<new_id> and seeing role='admin'

trigger: Any authenticated non-admin user sends DELETE /api/menu/1

triggers.description: Request uses DELETE method on a menu endpoint
Delete endpoint returns 200 for a non-admin session (no role check)
Response confirms item removal without admin authorisation

validation: Response is 200 with 'item removed from menu'. Menu item id=1 no longer appears in GET /api/menu

trigger: Cross-origin POST /api/orders with valid session cookie and Content-Type: application/json

triggers.description: Request includes a cross-origin Origin header (simulating CSRF attack)
Order placed successfully despite cross-origin request (no CSRF token checked)
Response contains order_id confirming order was placed without CSRF token

validation: Order is placed successfully without any CSRF token validation

triggers.description: File name parameter contains directory traversal sequences
Response contains Go module declaration (go.mod read outside uploads/)
Response contains Unix/Linux system file content indicators

validation: Response returns content of go.mod (or any readable file outside uploads/)

triggers.description: Cart response echoes back attacker-controlled discount and promo fields without recalculation
Response contains staff-only promo code or manipulated zero total (trust of client data)

validation: Response echoes back the deserialized cart including attacker-controlled discount/total fields without server-side recalculation

detection_hints: Upload a non-image extension such as .html or .svg
Confirm the response returns a public_url under /static/uploads/menus/
Fetch the uploaded asset directly to verify storage/exposure
trigger: POST /api/admin/menu/upload-image with multipart file named shell.svg or menu-admin.html

triggers.description: Upload endpoint receives multipart file data
Upload response returns the public URL of the stored asset
Uploaded asset is exposed from the public static uploads directory

validation: Any uploaded file type is accepted, written under ./static/uploads/menus, and returned as a publicly fetchable URL

detection_hints: Use a staff token to set stock negative or to an unrealistic value
Observe that no approval workflow, range check, or warehouse validation exists
Read the updated stock back from /api/kitchen/inventory

triggers.description: Inventory adjustment request targets the kitchen stock API
Adjusted inventory response contains negative or extreme stock values
Inventory API echoes back updated stock and location without enforcement

validation: The API accepts arbitrary direct stock rewrites, including negative values, without validation or workflow controls

type: Local File Inclusion / Remote File Inclusion
detection_hints: Use ../../ paths to read local files outside ./recipes
Use http:// or https:// to make the server fetch a remote recipe template
Look for resolved_path, content, or remote fetch metadata in the response

triggers.description: Recipe source contains traversal sequences or remote URL schemes
Response exposes local resolved_path or remote fetch metadata
Response body contains local file or internal service content

validation: The recipe viewer reads arbitrary local files via relative paths and fetches remote content directly when a URL is supplied

detection_hints: Send both Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding: chunked headers
Embed a second HTTP request after 0\r\n\r\n in the request body
Look for desync, smuggled_request, and interpretation mismatch fields in the response
trigger: POST /api/kitchen/dispatch with both Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding: chunked plus a second request after 0\r\n\r\n

triggers.description: Request contains both TE and CL headers, indicating a parser desynchronisation attempt
Dispatch response reports a desync and parsed smuggled request metadata
Response explicitly shows frontend/backend interpretation mismatch

validation: When both TE and CL are supplied, the dispatch handler simulates a frontend/backend parsing mismatch and exposes the smuggled internal request target

detection_hints: Export an invoice and inspect the returned temp_path/public_url
Guess another order's invoice filename under /static/exports/tmp/
Confirm the file persists until manual cleanup

triggers.description: Invoice export route is invoked
Export response returns predictable temporary file metadata
Temporary invoice is exposed under a public static path

validation: Invoice exports are written to a predictable public static directory with no automatic cleanup, making temporary files guessable and accessible

detection_hints: Forge a token with alg=none and role=admin
Reuse an expired token; exp is ignored by the panel endpoints
Modify the JWT role claim and sign with the weak hardcoded secret
trigger: GET /api/staff/panel with a forged JWT containing role=admin and alg=none

triggers.description: Staff panel request carries a bearer token
Staff panel still returns success for a forged or expired token
Panel response exposes authenticated role and inventory data

validation: The staff panel accepts alg=none tokens, uses a weak hardcoded signing secret, and does not validate expiration or issuer claims
